---
# Situation
A reversible decision is treated as final, so analysis replaces action.

# Approaches Tried
Set a deadline; write decision criteria; name the concrete fears.

# What Worked
A fixed deadline + written criteria converts dread into a choice.

# What Didn't
Open-ended "more research" deepened the avoidance.

# Lesson / Principle
Most decisions are reversible; speed beats certainty past a point.

# Source & Attribution
Stoic practice; common decision-coaching guidance.
